steps.title: Set the function equal to zero
steps.content: To find the x‑intercepts, set $$y=0$$. Since the constant factor 3 is not zero, we have $$(x-14)(x+5)(x+4)=0$$.

steps.title: Solve each factor for x
steps.content: Setting each factor equal to zero gives $$x-14=0$$ → $$x=14$$, $$x+5=0$$ → $$x=-5$$, and $$x+4=0$$ → $$x=-4$$. Therefore the x‑intercepts occur at $$x=14$$, $$x=-5$$, and $$x=-4$$; one of them is $$x=14$$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
